input_prompt: A platform for campus clubs to manage events, recruit members, and collect dues online

contract.tagline: The all-in-one OS for campus organizations.
contract.problem_statement: Campus clubs manage events on Google Forms, collect dues with Venmo, and recruit via flyers. There is no unified platform causing 40% of dues to go uncollected and 60% of event signups to be no-shows. Existing tools like Eventbrite or Mailchimp are too expensive and too generic for student-run organizations operating on $0 budgets.
target_users: Club presidents at universities with 50-500 member organizations
University student government associations overseeing 50+ clubs
Faculty advisors tracking student organization compliance

core_features.name: Smart Dues Collection
core_features.description: Automated recurring dues with Stripe, auto-reminders at 3/7/14 days, and instant treasurer dashboard.
core_features.user_story: As a treasurer, I want dues collected automatically so that I stop chasing 200 members on Venmo.

core_features.name: Event Management Engine
core_features.description: RSVP tracking, waitlists, QR check-in codes, and post-event attendance reports.
core_features.user_story: As a club president, I want to track event attendance so that I can report to university administration.

core_features.name: Member Recruitment Pipeline
core_features.description: Custom interest forms, automated follow-up emails, and a Kanban-style member onboarding board.
core_features.user_story: As a recruitment chair, I want to track prospective members so that no lead falls through the cracks.

core_features.name: Treasury & Budget Tracker
core_features.description: Income/expense ledger, budget categories, approval workflows for reimbursements.
core_features.user_story: As a treasurer, I want a real budget tracker so that I can present financials to student government.

core_features.name: University SSO Integration
core_features.description: One-click login with university .edu accounts via SAML/OAuth so no separate signup is needed.
core_features.user_story: As a student, I want to log in with my university account so that I don't need another password.

core_features.name: Communication Hub
core_features.description: Segmented email blasts, SMS announcements, and a member-facing club portal.
core_features.user_story: As a president, I want to send targeted messages so that announcements reach the right members.

steps: Signs up with university .edu SSO in 30 seconds
Creates club profile and invites 3 officer co-admins
Sets up $50/semester dues collection with Stripe in 2 minutes
Creates first event with RSVP form and QR check-in
Sends recruitment blast to 300 club fair contacts
user_journeys.success_condition: Club is fully operational on ClubFlow within 15 minutes of signing up

steps: Receives ClubFlow university license proposal from student government
Reviews compliance with FERPA via SOC 2 report
Approves platform-wide SSO integration with university IdP
Monitors all 200 campus clubs from single admin dashboard
user_journeys.success_condition: University selects ClubFlow as official student organization platform

tech_stack.frontend: Next.js 14, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI
tech_stack.backend: FastAPI (Python), Celery for async tasks, Redis
tech_stack.auth: NextAuth.js with SAML 2.0 university SSO + Stripe

api_endpoints.description: List all club members with dues status
Trigger dues collection via Stripe for all members
Get full treasury ledger for the club

contract.revenue_model: Freemium SaaS with B2B2C university licensing. Free tier drives adoption at club level; revenue comes from Club Pro ($29/mo) and enterprise University licenses ($499/mo per institution). Target: 500 university deals at $499/mo = $3M ARR.
contract.go_to_market: Bottom-up through student government associations. Partner directly with NASPA (student affairs professionals network). Sponsor 10 large university club fairs. Referral program: clubs that refer 5 other clubs get 3 months free.
known_risks: University IT procurement cycles can be 12-18 months — slow to close enterprise deals
Students change annually, creating high leadership churn and re-onboarding costs
Free Stripe tier has transaction fees that eat margin at small dues amounts (<$20)
University IT may mandate their own payment processor, blocking Stripe integration

generated_files.backend/main.py: # ClubFlow FastAPI backend — generated by PROMETHEUS
generated_files.README.md:
# ClubFlow

The all-in-one OS for campus organizations.

kill_reports.title: University Procurement Kills Runway
kill_reports.description: B2B university sales cycles average 14 months. With a $499/mo enterprise price, you need 50+ university deals to hit $300K ARR — that's 2+ years of sales motion before meaningful revenue. Meanwhile Club Pro at $29/mo requires 862 clubs to hit $300K. Neither path reaches ramen profitability without significant capital. Mailchimp solved this by charging per-email to monetize the free tier immediately.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Add a 2.5% transaction fee on all dues collected via the free tier immediately. At $50/semester × 100 members × 1,000 clubs = $125K ARR from day one, no sales cycle required.
kill_reports.patch_description: Added 2.5% platform fee on all dues transactions processed via the free tier, creating immediate revenue from launch without requiring enterprise sales cycles.

kill_reports.title: FERPA Violation: Exposing PII Without DPA
kill_reports.description: Storing student .edu email addresses, club membership data, and payment history constitutes FERPA-protected educational records. Without a signed Data Processing Agreement with each university and a BAA for any subprocessors (Supabase, Stripe), the platform is in violation before the first user signs up. FERPA violations carry institutional-level fines and can result in the university losing federal funding.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Add DPA template to university onboarding flow. Implement data residency controls per institution. Add student-facing data deletion request endpoint (FERPA Right to Review). Conduct SOC 2 Type I within 6 months.
kill_reports.patch_description: Implemented automated DPA signing flow in university onboarding, added per-institution data isolation at the database row level, and created FERPA data request API endpoint at GET /api/students/{id}/data-export.

kill_reports.title: Zero Viral Loop — All Acquisition is Manual
kill_reports.description: The current GTM relies entirely on manually attending club fairs and cold outreach to NASPA. There is no product-led viral mechanism. When a club sends a dues request or event invite to 200 members, each of those members should see ClubFlow branding and be able to start their own club. Notion, Figma, and Linear all grew virally through the documents and projects users shared externally.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Add 'Powered by ClubFlow' branding with clickable link on all member-facing emails, event pages, and payment receipts. Add one-click 'Start your own club' CTA on member portal. A/B test virality coefficient.
kill_reports.patch_description: Added persistent 'Powered by ClubFlow' branding with recruitment CTA on all outbound member communications, event RSVP pages, and payment confirmation screens to activate a PLG viral loop.

kill_reports.title: PCI-DSS Scope Expansion via Direct Card Handling
kill_reports.description: Even with Stripe, if the frontend ever touches raw card data (custom card inputs vs. Stripe Elements), the entire infrastructure enters PCI-DSS Level 1 scope — requiring a Qualified Security Assessor audit costing $20,000-$50,000/year. The current architecture does not explicitly enforce Stripe Elements-only card capture.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Enforce Stripe Elements/Payment Links exclusively. Add CSP headers blocking any non-Stripe payment iframes. Document PCI-DSS SAQ-A compliance posture in security policy.
kill_reports.patch_description: Enforced Stripe.js Payment Elements for all card capture, added Content-Security-Policy headers blocking third-party payment iframes, and documented SAQ-A compliance attestation.

kill_reports.title: Dues Collection Creates Stripe API Thundering Herd
kill_reports.description: When a treasurer clicks 'Collect Dues', the system fans out a Stripe PaymentIntent for every member simultaneously. A club with 500 members hitting Stripe at once will hit Stripe's default rate limit of 100 req/s per account, causing 80% of payment attempts to fail silently. This is a P0 failure for the core monetization flow.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Move dues collection to a Celery task queue with exponential backoff. Process 10 members/second max. Show real-time collection progress in the dashboard via WebSocket. Use Stripe Batch API when available.
kill_reports.patch_description: Moved dues collection to Celery beat scheduler with 10 req/s rate cap, implemented real-time progress WebSocket updates in the treasurer dashboard, and added idempotency keys on all PaymentIntent creation calls.

kill_reports.title: Annual Student Churn Destroys Retention Metrics
kill_reports.description: Club leadership turns over every academic year. The president who onboarded ClubFlow graduates and the incoming president has never heard of it. This creates effective 40-70% annual churn at the admin user level — the exact users who pay the bill. Campuslabs (acquired by Anthology) solved this by embedding into the university IT stack, not the club.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Build a 'Succession Toolkit' — automatic leadership transition flow, institutional memory export, and a faculty advisor dashboard so the stable university stakeholder (not the graduating president) owns the relationship.
kill_reports.patch_description: Built a Succession Toolkit featuring auto-detecting leadership transition prompts at semester end, one-click admin transfer with full history preservation, and a permanent faculty advisor co-admin role anchoring the account to a stable institutional stakeholder.

kill_reports.title: No Offline Check-in Fallback for Poor WiFi Venues
kill_reports.description: QR check-in requires internet connectivity. University event venues (gyms, outdoor spaces, old lecture halls) frequently have poor WiFi. A club doing a 300-person event with no offline fallback will have a line disaster and blame ClubFlow, not the WiFi.
kill_reports.suggested_fix: Implement a Progressive Web App (PWA) with offline check-in via IndexedDB sync. Pre-download attendee list before event. Sync check-ins when connectivity restores.
kill_reports.challenge_argument: Every major venue platform including Eventbrite and Hopin launched without offline mode and it was not a retention-breaking issue. University WiFi has improved dramatically. PWA offline sync adds 3 months of development for a problem affecting less than 5% of events. We will ship this in v1.1 based on real user feedback, not speculation.
